phrases.def: 哲人王（柏拉图理想中的统治者）

sentences.en: Looked at in one way, everyone knows what intelligence is; looked at in another way, no one does.
sentences.zh: 从一个角度看，人人都知道智力是什么；从另一个角度看，却无人知晓。

grammar.type: 过去分词状语 + 分号对比
grammar.note: 两分句由分号连接，形成对比；Looked at in one way / in another way 均为过去分词短语作条件状语；后句 no one does 中 does 替代 knows what intelligence is。

words.def: 从一个角度；在某种程度上

sentences.en: In other words, people all have unconscious notions – known as ‘implicit theories’ – of intelligence, but no one knows for certain what it actually is.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，人们对智力都有一些无意识的观念——即所谓的“内隐理论”——但没有人确切知道它究竟是什么。

grammar.type: 破折号插入(过去分词) + but 转折
grammar.note: 主干 people all have unconscious notions... of intelligence；两破折号间 known as 'implicit theories' 为过去分词短语作插入定语；but no one knows for certain what it actually is，what... 为宾语从句。

sentences.en: This chapter addresses how people conceptualize intelligence, whatever it may actually be.
sentences.zh: 本章探讨的是人们如何构想智力这一概念——无论它实际上究竟是什么。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + whatever 让步从句
grammar.note: 主干 This chapter addresses how people conceptualize intelligence，how... 为宾语从句；whatever it may actually be 为 whatever 引导的让步状语从句。

sentences.en: But why should we even care what people think intelligence is, as opposed only to valuing whatever it actually is?
sentences.zh: 但我们为何还要在意人们认为智力是什么，而不是只看重它实际上是什么呢？

grammar.type: 特殊疑问句 + as opposed to 对比
grammar.note: 主句为疑问句 why should we even care what people think intelligence is，what... 为宾语从句；as opposed only to valuing whatever it actually is 为 as opposed to 引导的对比状语，其后接动名词 valuing。

sentences.en: There are at least four reasons people’s conceptions of intelligence matter.
sentences.zh: 人们对智力的看法之所以重要，至少有四个原因。

grammar.type: there be + 省略关系词定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 There are at least four reasons；(why) people's conceptions of intelligence matter 为省略关系副词的定语从句修饰 reasons，matter 为不及物动词“要紧、重要”。

sentences.en: First, implicit theories of intelligence drive the way in which people perceive and evaluate their own intelligence and that of others.
sentences.zh: 第一，关于智力的内隐理论支配着人们感知和评价自身智力及他人智力的方式。

grammar.type: in which 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 implicit theories of intelligence drive the way；in which people perceive and evaluate their own intelligence and that of others 为定语从句修饰 the way；that 替代 the intelligence。

sentences.en: To better understand the judgments people make about their own and others’ abilities, it is useful to learn about people’s implicit theories.
sentences.zh: 为了更好地理解人们对自己和他人能力所作的判断，了解人们的内隐理论是有益的。

grammar.type: 不定式状语 + 形式主语
grammar.note: To better understand the judgments (that) people make about... 为不定式作目的状语，含省略关系词定语从句；主句 it is useful to learn about people's implicit theories，it 为形式主语。

sentences.en: For example, parents’ implicit theories of their children’s language development will determine at what ages they will be willing to make various corrections in their children’s speech.
sentences.zh: 例如，父母对孩子语言发展的内隐理论，会决定他们愿意在孩子几岁时对其言语进行各种纠正。

grammar.type: 宾语从句(at what ages)
grammar.note: 主干 parents' implicit theories... will determine at what ages...，at what ages they will be willing to make various corrections 为 determine 的宾语从句（疑问词短语引导）。

sentences.en: More generally, parents’ implicit theories of intelligence will determine at what ages they believe their children are ready to perform various cognitive tasks.
sentences.zh: 更普遍地说，父母对智力的内隐理论，会决定他们认为孩子在几岁时能够胜任各种认知任务。

grammar.type: 宾语从句嵌套
grammar.note: 主干 parents' implicit theories... will determine at what ages...；从句内 they believe their children are ready to perform various cognitive tasks，believe 后又接宾语从句。

sentences.en: Job interviewers will make hiring decisions on the basis of their implicit theories of intelligence. People will decide who to be friends with on the basis of such theories.
sentences.zh: 招聘面试官会依据其对智力的内隐理论来做出录用决定。人们也会依据这类理论来决定与谁交朋友。

grammar.type: on the basis of 状语
grammar.note: 两句结构相似，on the basis of... 均为方式状语，意为“以……为依据”；后句 who to be friends with 为“疑问词+不定式”作 decide 的宾语。

sentences.en: In sum, knowledge about implicit theories of intelligence is important because this knowledge is so often used by people to make judgments in the course of their everyday lives.
sentences.zh: 总之，了解关于智力的内隐理论很重要，因为人们在日常生活中常常运用这种知识来做出判断。

grammar.type: 原因状语从句 + 被动语态
grammar.note: 主句 knowledge about implicit theories of intelligence is important；because this knowledge is so often used by people to make judgments 为原因状语从句（被动）；in the course of their everyday lives 为时间状语。

sentences.en: Second, the implicit theories of scientific investigators ultimately give rise to their explicit theories.
sentences.zh: 第二，科学研究者的内隐理论最终会催生出他们的外显理论。

grammar.type: give rise to 结构
grammar.note: 主干 the implicit theories of scientific investigators ultimately give rise to their explicit theories；give rise to 意为“引起、产生”。

sentences.en: Thus it is useful to find out what these implicit theories are.
sentences.zh: 因此，弄清这些内隐理论究竟是什么是有益的。

grammar.type: 形式主语 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: it is useful to find out what these implicit theories are，it 为形式主语，to find out... 为真正主语，what these implicit theories are 为宾语从句。

sentences.en: Implicit theories provide a framework that is useful in defining the general scope of a phenomenon – especially a not-well-understood phenomenon.
sentences.zh: 内隐理论提供了一个框架，有助于界定某一现象——尤其是尚未被充分理解的现象——的大致范围。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 破折号补充
grammar.note: 主干 Implicit theories provide a framework；that is useful in defining the general scope of a phenomenon 为定语从句修饰 framework；破折号后 especially a not-well-understood phenomenon 为对 phenomenon 的补充强调。

sentences.en: These implicit theories can suggest what aspects of the phenomenon have been more or less attended to in previous investigations.
sentences.zh: 这些内隐理论可以提示：在以往的研究中，该现象的哪些方面受到了较多或较少的关注。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + 现在完成时被动
grammar.note: 主干 These implicit theories can suggest what aspects...；what aspects of the phenomenon have been more or less attended to 为宾语从句（现在完成时被动），attend to 意为“关注、处理”。

sentences.en: Third, implicit theories can be useful when an investigator suspects that existing explicit theories are wrong or misleading.
sentences.zh: 第三，当研究者怀疑现有的外显理论有误或具有误导性时，内隐理论会很有用。

grammar.type: when 时间状语从句 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: 主句 implicit theories can be useful；when an investigator suspects that... 为时间状语从句，that existing explicit theories are wrong or misleading 为 suspects 的宾语从句。

sentences.en: If an investigation of implicit theories reveals little correspondence between the extant implicit and explicit theories, the implicit theories may be wrong.
sentences.zh: 如果对内隐理论的调查显示，现存的内隐理论与外显理论之间几乎没有对应关系，那么这些内隐理论可能是错误的。

grammar.type: if 条件状语从句
grammar.note: If an investigation... reveals little correspondence between... 为条件状语从句；主句 the implicit theories may be wrong；extant 意为“现存的”，correspondence 意为“对应、一致”。

sentences.en: But the possibility also needs to be taken into account that the explicit theories are wrong and in need of correction or supplementation.
sentences.zh: 但也需要考虑到另一种可能：外显理论本身是错误的，需要加以纠正或补充。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 同位语从句后置
grammar.note: 主干 the possibility also needs to be taken into account（被动，take into account 意为“考虑”）；that the explicit theories are wrong and in need of correction or supplementation 为同位语从句，因主语过长而后置于 account 之后。

sentences.en: For example, some implicit theories of intelligence suggest the need for expansion of some of our explicit theories of the construct.
sentences.zh: 例如，一些关于智力的内隐理论表明，我们对这一概念的某些外显理论有加以扩展的必要。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + of 后置定语
grammar.note: 主干 some implicit theories of intelligence suggest the need for expansion；of some of our explicit theories of the construct 为后置定语说明扩展的对象，construct 此处意为“（理论）概念、构念”。

sentences.en: Finally, understanding implicit theories of intelligence can help elucidate developmental and cross-cultural differences.
sentences.zh: 最后，理解关于智力的内隐理论，有助于阐明发展方面和跨文化方面的差异。

grammar.type: 动名词主语 + help do
grammar.note: 主语为动名词短语 understanding implicit theories of intelligence；谓语 can help elucidate...，help (to) do 结构；developmental and cross-cultural differences 为并列宾语。

sentences.en: As mentioned earlier, people have expectations for intellectual performances that differ for children of different ages.
sentences.zh: 如前所述，人们对不同年龄儿童的智力表现抱有不同的期望。

grammar.type: As 状语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: As mentioned earlier 为 as 引导的方式状语（省略）；主干 people have expectations for intellectual performances；that differ for children of different ages 为定语从句修饰 expectations/performances。

sentences.en: How these expectations differ is in part a function of culture.
sentences.zh: 这些期望如何各不相同，在一定程度上是文化的产物。

grammar.type: 主语从句 + a function of
grammar.note: 主语为 How these expectations differ（主语从句）；谓语 is in part a function of culture，a function of 此处意为“取决于……、由……决定的产物”。

sentences.en: For example, expectations for children who participate in Western-style schooling are almost certain to be different from those for children who do not participate in such schooling.
sentences.zh: 例如，对接受西式学校教育的儿童的期望，几乎必然不同于对不接受此类教育的儿童的期望。

grammar.type: 两个 who 定语从句 + 比较
grammar.note: 主干 expectations for children... are almost certain to be different from those for children...；两个 who 定语从句分别修饰前后的 children；those 替代 expectations。

sentences.en: I have suggested that there are three major implicit theories of how intelligence relates to society as a whole (Sternberg, 1997).
sentences.zh: 我曾提出，关于智力如何与整个社会相关联，存在三种主要的内隐理论（斯滕伯格，1997）。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + how 从句
grammar.note: 主干 I have suggested that...；从句 there are three major implicit theories of how intelligence relates to society as a whole，how... 为介词宾语从句作 theories 的后置定语；as a whole 意为“作为整体”。

sentences.en: These might be called Hamiltonian, Jeffersonian, and Jacksonian.
sentences.zh: 这三种理论或可称为汉密尔顿式、杰斐逊式和杰克逊式。

grammar.type: 被动语态
grammar.note: 主干 These might be called Hamiltonian, Jeffersonian, and Jacksonian（被动，call sth + 名称）；三个并列表语说明其名称。

sentences.en: These views are not based strictly, but rather, loosely, on the philosophies of Alexander Hamilton, Thomas Jefferson, and Andrew Jackson, three great statesmen in the history of the United States.
sentences.zh: 这些观点并非严格地、而是宽泛地建立在亚历山大·汉密尔顿、托马斯·杰斐逊和安德鲁·杰克逊——美国历史上三位伟大的政治家——的思想之上。

grammar.type: not...but rather 结构 + 同位语
grammar.note: 主干 These views are not based strictly, but rather, loosely, on the philosophies of...，not... but rather... 构成对比，strictly 与 loosely 对照；three great statesmen... 为前述三人名的同位语。

sentences.en: The Hamiltonian view, which is similar to the Platonic view, is that people are born with different levels of intelligence and that those who are less intelligent need the good offices of the more intelligent to keep them in line, whether they are called government officials or, in Plato’s term, philosopher-kings.
sentences.zh: 汉密尔顿式的观点与柏拉图的观点相似，认为人生来就具有不同水平的智力，而智力较低者需要更聪明者的帮助来约束自己——无论那些更聪明者被称为政府官员，还是用柏拉图的说法称为“哲人王”。

grammar.type: 非限制性定语从句 + 表语从句并列 + whether 让步
grammar.note: which is similar to the Platonic view 为非限制性定语从句；主干 The Hamiltonian view... is that... and that...，两个 that 引导并列表语从句；whether they are called government officials or... philosopher-kings 为 whether...or... 让步状语；keep sb in line 意为“约束、使守规矩”。

sentences.en: Herrnstein and Murray (1994) seem to have shared this belief when they wrote about the emergence of a cognitive (high-IQ) elite, which eventually would have to take responsibility for the largely irresponsible masses of non-elite (low-IQ) people who cannot take care of themselves.
sentences.zh: 赫恩斯坦和默里（1994）在论及一个认知（高智商）精英阶层的兴起时，似乎也持有这一信念——这个精英阶层最终将不得不为那些基本上无力自理、不负责任的非精英（低智商）大众承担责任。

grammar.type: when 时间从句 + 非限制性定语从句 + who 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 Herrnstein and Murray seem to have shared this belief；when they wrote about the emergence of a cognitive elite 为时间状语从句；which eventually would have to take responsibility for... 为非限制性定语从句修饰 elite；who cannot take care of themselves 为定语从句修饰 people。

sentences.en: Left to themselves, the unintelligent would create, as they always have created, a kind of chaos.
sentences.zh: 若任其自行其是，智力低下者就会像他们一贯所做的那样，制造出某种混乱。

grammar.type: 过去分词状语 + 插入语
grammar.note: Left to themselves 为过去分词短语作条件状语，相当于 If left to themselves；主句 the unintelligent would create... a kind of chaos；as they always have created 为 as 引导的插入方式状语。

words.def: 任其自行其是；放任不管

sentences.en: The Jeffersonian view is that people should have equal opportunities, but they do not necessarily avail themselves equally of these opportunities and are not necessarily equally rewarded for their accomplishments.
sentences.zh: 杰斐逊式的观点认为，人们应当拥有平等的机会，但他们未必会同等地利用这些机会，也未必因其成就而获得同等的回报。

grammar.type: 表语从句 + but 并列
grammar.note: 主干 The Jeffersonian view is that...；表语从句内 people should have equal opportunities, but they do not necessarily avail themselves... and are not necessarily equally rewarded...，but 引出转折，两谓语并列；avail oneself of 意为“利用”。

sentences.en: People are rewarded for what they accomplish, if given equal opportunity. Low achievers are not rewarded to the same extent as high achievers.
sentences.zh: 在被给予平等机会的前提下，人们会因其所取得的成就而获得回报。低成就者所获得的回报不及高成就者。

grammar.type: 省略式条件状语 + 比较结构
grammar.note: 首句 People are rewarded for what they accomplish，if given equal opportunity 为省略主语和 be 的条件状语从句；后句 Low achievers are not rewarded to the same extent as high achievers，to the same extent as 意为“与……程度相同”。

words.def: 有成就的人；取得成绩者

sentences.en: In the Jeffersonian view, the goal of education is not to favor or foster an elite, as in the Hamiltonian tradition, but rather to allow children the opportunities to make full use of the skills they have.
sentences.zh: 在杰斐逊式的观点中，教育的目标不是像汉密尔顿传统那样偏袒或培养精英，而是让孩子有机会充分发挥他们所拥有的技能。

grammar.type: not...but rather 结构 + 省略关系词定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 the goal of education is not to favor or foster an elite... but rather to allow children the opportunities...，not... but rather... 连接两个不定式；as in the Hamiltonian tradition 为插入类比；the skills (that) they have 为省略关系词定语从句。

sentences.en: My own views are similar to these (Sternberg, 1997).
sentences.zh: 我本人的观点与这些观点相似（斯滕伯格，1997）。

grammar.type: 主系表
grammar.note: 简单句，主干 My own views are similar to these；these 指代前述杰斐逊式的观点。

sentences.en: The Jacksonian view is that all people are equal, not only as human beings but in terms of their competencies – that one person would serve as well as another in government or on a jury or in almost any position of responsibility.
sentences.zh: 杰克逊式的观点认为，所有人都是平等的，不仅作为人是平等的，在能力方面也是平等的——即无论在政府中、陪审团里，还是几乎任何担负责任的岗位上，一个人都能和另一个人做得一样好。

grammar.type: 表语从句 + not only...but... + 破折号同位
grammar.note: 主干 The Jacksonian view is that all people are equal；not only as human beings but in terms of their competencies 为 not only...but... 并列状语；破折号后 that one person would serve as well as another... 为对前述内容的同位/解释从句。

sentences.en: In this view of democracy, people are essentially intersubstitutable except for specialized skills, all of which can be learned.
sentences.zh: 在这种民主观中，除了专门技能之外，人与人本质上是可以相互替代的，而所有这些专门技能都是可以习得的。

grammar.type: except for 状语 + 非限制性定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 people are essentially intersubstitutable；except for specialized skills 为例外状语；all of which can be learned 为非限制性定语从句修饰 specialized skills。

sentences.en: In this view, we do not need or want any institutions that might lead to favoring one group over another.
sentences.zh: 在这种观点看来，我们既不需要、也不希望有任何可能导致偏袒某一群体而轻视另一群体的制度。

grammar.type: 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 we do not need or want any institutions；that might lead to favoring one group over another 为定语从句修饰 institutions，favor A over B 意为“偏袒A胜过B”。

sentences.en: Implicit theories of intelligence and of the relationship of intelligence to society perhaps need to be considered more carefully than they have been because they often serve as underlying presuppositions for explicit theories and even experimental designs that are then taken as scientific contributions.
sentences.zh: 关于智力、以及智力与社会之关系的内隐理论，或许需要比以往更审慎地加以考量，因为它们往往充当着外显理论乃至实验设计的潜在预设，而这些外显理论和实验设计随后又被当作科学贡献。

grammar.type: 比较结构 + 原因从句 + 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 Implicit theories... need to be considered more carefully than they have been，than 引出比较；because they often serve as underlying presuppositions for... 为原因状语从句；that are then taken as scientific contributions 为定语从句修饰 explicit theories and even experimental designs。

sentences.en: Until scholars are able to discuss their implicit theories and thus their assumptions, they are likely to miss the point of what others are saying when discussing their explicit theories and their data.
sentences.zh: 在学者们能够讨论各自的内隐理论、从而讨论各自的假设之前，他们在讨论彼此的外显理论和数据时，很可能会误解对方的本意。

grammar.type: Until 时间状语从句 + 宾语从句 + 省略式时间状语
grammar.note: Until scholars are able to discuss... 为 until 引导的时间状语从句；主句 they are likely to miss the point of what others are saying，what... 为宾语从句；when discussing their explicit theories and their data 为省略主语的时间状语从句。

questions.title: Questions 1–3
instructions: Reading Passage 1 has ten sections, A–J.
Which section contains the following information?
Write the correct letter, A–J, in boxes 1–3 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt: information about how non-scientists’ assumptions about intelligence influence their behaviour towards others
a reference to lack of clarity over the definition of intelligence
the point that a researcher’s implicit and explicit theories may be very different

questions.title: Questions 4–6
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 4–6 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

items.prompt: Slow language development in children is likely to prove disappointing to their parents.
People’s expectations of what children should gain from education are universal.
Scholars may discuss theories without fully understanding each other.

questions.title: Questions 7–13
instructions: Look at the following statements (Questions 7–13) and the list of theories below.
Match each statement with the correct theory, A, B, or C.
Write the correct letter, A, B, or C, in boxes 7–13 on your answer sheet.
NB You may use any letter more than once.

items.prompt: It is desirable for the same possibilities to be open to everyone.
No section of society should have preferential treatment at the expense of another.
People should only gain benefits on the basis of what they actually achieve.
Variation in intelligence begins at birth.
The more intelligent people should be in positions of power.
Everyone can develop the same abilities.
People of low intelligence are likely to lead uncontrolled lives.
